title: Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip
Identify key diagnostic features of Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip
Apply clinical reasoning to Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip management
Recognize common pitfalls in Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip

sections.title: Overview of Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip
sections.content:
**Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip**



**Key Points:**
• Spectrum: subluxation → dislocatable → dislocated → teratologic
• Barlow (provocative): adducts and pushes posteriorly — dislocates an unstable hip
• Ortolani (reduction): abducts and lifts anteriorly — reduces a dislocated hip (the 'clunk')

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Spectrum: subluxation → dislocatable → dislocated → teratologic
• Barlow (provocative): adducts and pushes posteriorly — dislocates an unstable hip

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Ortolani (reduction): abducts and lifts anteriorly — reduces a dislocated hip (the 'clunk')
• Risk factors: female, breech, firstborn, family history, oligohydramnios
• Treatment: Pavlik harness (0-6 months), closed/open reduction if Pavlik fails or diagnosed late

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip:**

• Ortolani (reduction): abducts and lifts anteriorly — reduces a dislocated hip (the 'clunk')
• Risk factors: female, breech, firstborn, family history, oligohydramnios
• Treatment: Pavlik harness (0-6 months), closed/open reduction if Pavlik fails or diagnosed late

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Developmental Dysplasia of the Hip?

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Spectrum: subluxation → dislocatable → dislocated → teratologic.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Spectrum: subluxation → dislocatable → dislocated → teratologic

coachDx.misconception: While typically self-resolving without treatment may seem plausible, it does not match the clinical scenario described.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Spectrum: subluxation → dislocatable → dislocated → teratologic from Typically self-resolving without treatment.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Spectrum: subluxation → dislocatable → dislocated → teratologic.
